text: The surgeon performs a totally laparoscopic pylorus-preserving gastrectomy, carefully dissecting the infrapyloric vessels and right gastroepiploic vessels. The right gastroepiploic vessels are ligated after preserving the infrapyloric vessels. The greater curvature is cleared without injuring the adjacent infrapyloric vessels.

The surgeon clears the lesser curvature near the anticipated resection area, followed by resection of the distal end of the stomach, located approximately five to six centimeters from the pylorus. Dissection of the suprapancreatic lymph node is performed above the common hepatic artery, avoiding injury to the right gastric artery. The coronary vein is identified, dissected, and firmly ligated. Suprapancreatic lymph node dissection continues above the splenic artery, preserving the celiac branch of the vagus nerve. Dissection along the left gastric artery is performed, with small branches to the liver ligated. The vagus nerve is identified, and white fibers behind the left gastric artery lymph node are dissected. The left gastric artery is divided and ligated, followed by dissection of the number three lymph node area towards the lesser curvature of the stomach.

The surgeon marks the proximal gastric resection line and transects the stomach. A hole is created on the greater curvature side of the distal stomach, and part of the staple line is opened. Another hole is made on the proximal stomach's greater curvature. A suture is placed on the staple line of the proximal stomach for traction. A hole is ensured on the lesser curvature side of the distal stomach for the piercing method. The linear stapler is inserted into both the proximal and distal stomachs, with the anvil piercing the hole on the lesser curvature staple line of the distal stomach. The stapler lines are gathered, and the distal part is cut off to remove avascular sections. Sutures are placed on the greater and lesser curvatures for traction. The entry hole of the gastrogastronomy, including avascular parts, is closed with a linear stapler. Intraoperative endoscopy is performed to ensure the safety of the anastomosis.
